title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 5 - A Look at the Core Sets (Part Four)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZ7RRiEw54E

Daniel (@dungeondive) surveys the card decks that form the engine of Shadows of Brimstone, from the dread-inducing darkness cards to the loot that keeps you coming back.

Two decks govern misfortune: the Darkness deck delivers immediate bad events during play, while the Growing Dread deck quietly accumulates face-down cards that all resolve at once when you reach your objective room—a brilliantly tense mechanic. The art across both decks is highlighted as some of the game's best. Gear cards provide the mundane loot layer—ropes, dynamite, dusty ponchos—while each otherworld has its own artifact deck full of themed treasures. The Belly of the Beast artifacts are a particular favourite, all biological horror: bile sacs, marrow blades, flesh armour, and nero sheaths.

Daniel explains how world cards add environmental rules to each setting (frigid snow on the Targa Plateau, patrol markers in Trederra) and how threat cards scale enemy encounters from green through to epic boss fights. His organisational approach resurfaces: he builds custom threat subsets so he only needs one box of miniatures per campaign. The episode reinforces a core appeal of Shadows of Brimstone—the sheer volume of randomised content that makes every session feel different.

----

What's your favourite loot moment in a dungeon crawl—the mundane gear that saves the day, or the epic artifact that changes everything?
